title: Lock Out And Tag Out The Operator Station From The Visu_Dual_MCP Screen
summary: Use the operator station HMI to open the Visu_Dual_MCP screen, stop the cycle with CYCLE STOP, optionally reposition the carriages using JOG and the axis +/- controls, then turn the operator station electrical panel power switch to OFF and apply lockout/tagout.
when_to_use: Use when the operator station must be placed into a stopped and locked/tagged out state from the Visu_Dual_MCP HMI workflow, including cases where carriage repositioning is needed before lockout/tagout.
not_for: Do not use as a basic operator procedure without site authorization.
Do not use when safe lockout/tagout execution requires details not present in this source excerpt.
Do not use this excerpt as the sole source for detailed jogging instructions; the source refers to "Jogging the Tipper" for those details.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the HMI stop, optional carriage repositioning, and operator station power-off and lockout/tagout steps.

safety_notes: If the operator approaches or extends their hand into the lifting connecting rod assembly while the robot is lifting, it may cause serious personal injury.
Be aware of the movement trajectory of this product, and make sure to not interfere with the line.
Do not touch the tippers while the system is powered ON.
This procedure includes hazardous energy isolation and powered equipment positioning.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI
Access to the operator station electrical panel

system_preconditions: Operator station is available for HMI interaction.
If carriage repositioning is required, the operator must use the documented JOG controls from the operator station HMI.
external_references: See "Jogging the Tipper" on page 67 for detailed jogging instructions.

visual_references.description: Operator station HMI screen for manually jogging the tipper axes during maintenance or positioning tasks.
Operator station HMI artifact associated with manually referencing tipper axes and navigating to Visu_Dual_MCP using F3.
Operator station HMI screen artifact associated with manually referencing axes and using Visu_Dual_MCP with CYCLE STOP.
Operator station control panel view showing CYCLE STOP, JOG, and related controls.

steps.title: Open the Visu_Dual_MCP screen
steps.purpose: Access the operator station HMI screen used for stopping the cycle and manual positioning before lockout/tagout.
steps.instruction: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct operator station HMI screen is open and the controls needed for stopping or jogging are available.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be reached or the expected controls are not available.

actions.label: Navigate to Visu_Dual_MCP
actions.exact_action: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station HMI context showing navigation to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen using F3.
Operator station HMI context associated with Visu_Dual_MCP and maintenance positioning.
Operator station screen used for manual jogging after entering the Visu_Dual_MCP workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be accessed.
The HMI does not present the expected stop or jog controls.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen.
On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen (F3).

steps.title: Stop the cycle
steps.purpose: Stop the automatic tipping cycle before any manual repositioning or lockout/tagout activity.
steps.expected_result: The automatic tipping cycle is stopped from the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The cycle is stopped and the station is no longer running the automatic tipping cycle.
steps.failure_condition: The cycle does not stop after pressing CYCLE STOP.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the CYCLE STOP control on the operator station control panel screen.
Operator station screen context used before entering JOG/manual movement.

stop_or_escalate_if: CYCLE STOP is unavailable on the HMI.
The automatic tipping cycle does not stop.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby text identifies CYCLE STOP controls for automatic operation.

steps.title: Enter jog mode if repositioning is needed
steps.purpose: Open the manual positioning controls only when carriage repositioning is required before lockout/tagout.
steps.instruction: If carriage repositioning is needed before lockout/tagout, press JOG.
steps.expected_result: The jogging/manual positioning screen is opened when repositioning is required.
steps.healthy_condition: JOG opens the manual movement controls for the tipper axes.
steps.failure_condition: JOG is needed but the jogging controls do not open.

actions.label: Press JOG
actions.exact_action: If you need to move the carriages to a different position, press JOG.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Manual mode/jogging screen with axis controls and plus/minus movement buttons.
Locate the JOG button used for manual positioning of the end effector and gripper.

stop_or_escalate_if: Repositioning is required but JOG cannot be accessed.
Safe repositioning requires details beyond those provided in this excerpt.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you need to move the carriages to a different position, press JOG.
Nearby text states operators should navigate to the Visu_Dual_MCP screen using F3, press CYCLE STOP, and then press JOG to open the jogging screen.

steps.title: Select the axis and reposition the carriages
steps.purpose: Move the carriages to the required position before power-off and lockout/tagout when repositioning is necessary.
steps.instruction: Select the corresponding axis for the tipper and use the + and - buttons to move the carriages. Refer to "Jogging the Tipper" for detailed jogging instructions.
steps.expected_result: The required carriage repositioning is completed using the documented axis selection and +/- controls.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected tipper axis responds as intended and the carriages reach the needed position without personnel interfering with the motion path.
steps.failure_condition: The axis cannot be selected, the carriage does not move as intended, or safe repositioning cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Select tipper axis
actions.exact_action: Select the corresponding axis for the tipper.

actions.label: Move carriage with plus/minus
actions.exact_action: Use the + and - buttons to move the carriages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis controls for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2 and the plus/minus buttons used to move the selected axis.

stop_or_escalate_if: Personnel would need to approach the lifting connecting rod assembly while the robot is lifting.
Personnel would interfere with the movement trajectory of the product or line.
Anyone would need to touch the tippers while the system is powered ON.
Detailed jogging guidance is required beyond the source excerpt.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Select the corresponding axis for the tipper and use the + and - buttons to move the carriages.
The manual mode interface includes Z1 AXIS and A1 AXIS controls for the left tipper, Z2 AXIS and A2 AXIS controls for the right tipper, and plus/minus buttons to move the selected axis.
If the operator approaches or extends their hand into the lifting connecting rod assembly while the robot is lifting, it may cause serious personal injury.
Be aware of the movement trajectory of this product, and make sure to not interfere with the line.
Do not touch the tippers while the system is powered ON.

steps.title: Turn the operator station power switch off and apply lockout/tagout
steps.purpose: Isolate power at the operator station electrical panel and place the station into a locked/tagged out state.
steps.instruction: At the operator station electrical panel, turn the power switch to "OFF" and apply lockout/tagout.
steps.expected_result: The operator station power switch is OFF and lockout/tagout has been applied.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station is in a stopped, power-isolated, locked/tagged out state.
steps.failure_condition: The power switch cannot be turned OFF, lockout/tagout cannot be applied, or safe isolation cannot be confirmed from the available source details.

actions.label: Turn power switch OFF
actions.exact_action: At the operator station electrical panel, turn the power switch to "OFF".

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator station electrical panel cannot be accessed.
The power switch cannot be turned to OFF.
Site-required lockout/tagout execution details are not available in this excerpt.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station electrical panel, turn the power switch to "OFF" and LOTO.

success_criteria: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen was used to stop the cycle with CYCLE STOP.
Any required carriage repositioning was completed using JOG, axis selection, and the + / - controls.
The operator station electrical panel power switch was turned to OFF.
Lockout/tagout was applied at the operator station.
healthy_conditions: The correct HMI screen is accessible.
CYCLE STOP successfully stops the automatic tipping cycle.
If needed, the selected axis and +/- controls allow controlled carriage repositioning.
The operator station power switch is OFF and the station is locked/tagged out.
failure_conditions: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be accessed.
CYCLE STOP does not stop the cycle.
JOG or axis movement controls are unavailable when repositioning is required.
Safe repositioning cannot be completed using the available source guidance.
The operator station power switch cannot be turned OFF or lockout/tagout cannot be applied.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if safe lockout/tagout execution requires details not present in this excerpt.
Escalate if detailed jogging instructions are needed beyond the source reference to "Jogging the Tipper."
Escalate if any step would require personnel to enter the motion path, approach the lifting connecting rod assembly during lifting, or touch the tippers while powered on.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Jog/manual mode screen with axis selection and plus/minus controls.
Visu_Dual_MCP navigation context on the operator station HMI.
Operator station HMI context for Visu_Dual_MCP and CYCLE STOP usage.
CYCLE STOP and JOG controls on the operator station control panel.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: 1.2: OPERATOR STATION LOCKOUT/TAGOUT: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen. Press CYCLE STOP. If you need to move the carriages to a different position, press JOG, select the corresponding axis for the tipper and use the + and - buttons to move the carriages. At the operator station electrical panel, turn the power switch to "OFF" and LOTO.
If the operator approaches or extends their hand into the lifting connecting rod assembly while the robot is lifting, it may cause serious personal injury.
Be aware of the movement trajectory of this product, and make sure to not interfere with the line.
Do not touch the tippers while the system is powered ON.

missing_details: The excerpt does not provide the detailed jogging instructions and instead refers to "Jogging the Tipper".
The excerpt does not provide a detailed lockout/tagout subprocedure beyond turning the power switch to OFF and applying LOTO.
No estimated completion time is provided in the source.
No explicit verification method for confirming zero energy state is provided in the supplied source packet.
